item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
u thought I was birbo
but was doggo all along
bamboozled again

+1, the test logs are creative and make this entry amazing

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What appeared to be a juvenile golden retriever (Canis lupus familiaris) in a hummingbird costume made of construction paper.

And upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have three qualms about it. The bird summoning/demanifesting was interesting, and the "potion of summon birds" title makes it more so. The weird birds is an additional thing and I don't know of there's a reason for it. The mental images are very amusing, though, and I see why it is so loved.

Qualm #2: Any sort of media is to be screened for references to SCP-1306? I see the reasoning, but the logistics of it, man… For a single, harmless skip that doesn't routinely show up in media?

#3: Testing ended because supply ran out? But you have the recipe!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As others have said this is a 'thing that summons weird versions of a thing' scip and yet hot damn if that experiment log doesn't elevate it to the best freaking one I've read yet. Absolutely fantastic job +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: According to this page, it's a "subclass" used on exactly one page (this one, to be specific), which means that it is "Safe-class and undergoing testing or investigation."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This article is just weird birds1. It's nothing but weird birds.

So how is it this funny?

+1

Footnotes
1. For a given value of bird, anyway.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Things that summon otherworldly things that are superficially similar to normal things but are in fact weird things" is an exceedingly well-worn trope on this site (in fairness, it was probably less so when this was written), and it's one that lives or dies on how well the otherworldly things are written.

I was ready to no-vote before I got to the golden retriever. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love this, especially the dog in the bird costume.

Safe-doctrina

What does this mean, though?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
it is i

i don't know whether i'll write anything else, i have an idea or two that i COULD execute but the real question is whether it's worth the effort and i'm still pondering that.

either way i don't think i have anything in me that looks anything like this. the potion of summon bird is a bit of a one-off.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is why we need Shrek back.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "When SCP-1306 is prepared and placed in an appropriate feeder outside of a building" - this reads awkwardly. Does it mean that the feeder needs to be in close proximity to a building, or does it mean that the feeder just has to be in an unenclosed space open to the atmosphere (so the middle of the Sahara Desert, hundreds of miles from any structure, would be fine?)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was looking for hummingbirds and found a dog. I can accept that.

…turned to directly face Research Assistant Jacobs (who was not visible from its position). Subject spoke the phrase "I bet you weren't expecting this, were you, Mikey?"

I was expecting his name to NOT be Mikey, actually.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
BIRD! BIRD! KILL IT! It's evil…
+1 for being so wonderfully bizarre.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Here

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I… I want more weird birds.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
… I'm pretty sure right now is the first time I noticed that.

._.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Goura scheepmakeri (Southern Crowned Pigeon)

You must have been pretty pleased with yourself when you found out about that one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I personally think the joke would get kind of worn out with a collaborative log, but if there's demand for it, I wouldn't automatically object to an unofficial supplement.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"I bet you weren't expecting this, were you, Mikey?" immediate upvote.

There should be a collaboration log of like "other testing" like they have for the eye of the forest.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You made me laugh in delighted glee at the puppy in a hummingbird costume - I'm such a child…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh my god this is amazing. I lost it at the ostrich. Ohhh, man this is amazing. Plus all of the ones.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So creative! Where do you come up with this shit? Test log was perfect length. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That log. Shear awesome. This is both funny enough to be a -J but awesome enough to mainlist. Excellent. I loved every entry.

I just can;t get over the image of the experimenters sitting in the monitoring booth making progressively more exhasperated exclamations of "WHAT."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nope. Fixed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Do you mean to say the recipe is only known to those three? Cause with this wording it almost sounds like you're saying the existence of the SCP itself is only known three people.
I've given up trying to figure out how much people will like something.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's a fair concern. I'll edit it in.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So, the best way they could come up with to keep the secret recipe safe is to have three guys memorize it? I could see strictly limiting access, since it is so easy to make. But wouldn't it make sense to at least write it down somehwere, just in case they all die in a containment breach, or something?

Nevermind, just my brain insisting I find at least one flaw in all good writing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Gene R gets a cookie.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So now you're giving us the birds and the bees?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wow. That is all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes please this is amazing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"What appeared to be a juvenile golden retriever (Canis lupus familiaris) in a hummingbird costume made of construction paper."

I think this is just perfect
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: These birds, they are incredible.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: And this is another in-universe trolling SCP. And you know what? I love it. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Initially I groaned at what looked like "yet another thing that summons things from other universes", but then I read the test log. And now I want more. :C

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's been a long time since I read something that was immediately, unpredictably delightful. Double bonus upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wasn't a huge fan of this until the observation log. The corpse of a bird levitating over to a feeder and limply shoving food into its decomposing mouth is too terrifying to not upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love everything about this skip. Personally, I had to stop and upvote at the ostrich, but that's just because there's very few things that'll make me stop to vote before I reach the end of an article. You had me at the cherenkov peacock.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I briefly considered making that one its own entry, but I decided it didn't have enough meat, and it would make a good addition to this article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have simplified them, and thanks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fixed, and danke schon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That third test log… zombie bird floating to this food… WEIRDEST MENTAL PICTURE EVER.

Subject was missing both legs, the majority of its bowels, and its left wing. There was no indication that the subject was alive.

I was like o___o

Subject floated towards the feeder through unknown means. A small amount of SCP-1306 was observed to leave the feeder and float into the subject's mouth. The subject then left in a similar manner.

but then I +1'd

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is certainly a curious little concept that made me smile. I'd upvote anything with a kiwi in it so start with, but those logs are just…wow. :P +1

Also, I see what you did there with the turkey.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is just crazy enough to work. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is incredible. Those logs made my day and my day's barely started. Have a +1 good sir.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was reading.

And then:

After four minutes, the subject's legs grew to a height of 2.3 meters.

And then:

Subject floated towards the feeder through unknown means. A small amount of SCP-1306 was observed to leave the feeder and float into the subject's mouth.

And then the retriever happened.
I tried to upvote more then once, yeah.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Besides the tone slips noted by Willjam, I had one stylistic quibble:

that are released to the general public that deal

Those nestled subordinate clauses are clunky as all hell, and could easily be simplified.

In other news, "birds per hour" is the best unit, and I commend you for finding a context where a puppy in a hummingbird costume almost makes sense. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Definitely an interesting concept few things I'd suggest looking into:

+ derp
- derp

"Instances of SCP-1306-1 generally do not correspond with any known birds" - they do seem to though, perhaps they display characteristics unseen in any birds of the same species?
"SCP-1306-1 do not exist prior to their appearance" seems a bit presumptuous. Perhaps "it is theorised." They could always be teleporting.
"containers that did not previously contain any birds." seems a bit specific, perhaps just "empty containers."
"any attempts made to contain them have resulted in the manifested instances being too large, strong, or fast to effectively contain." I feel as if that needs a bit more explanation.
Also just a few unscientific terms, "Liberal amount" of paint, "the subject took in", "grabbed".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I, too, loved the bird magic. It's already really interesting, to me, that the skip is kind of a magical potion found from non-amolous objects that makes weird shit happen. It's a pretty fun idea, and I dig weird birds. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked the skip itself, it's interesting, but I upvoted after the second entry in the observation log. It reads like the bird compensates for its inability to reach the feeder with the bird-magic. Go Avian Wizards.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I wrote this as an excuse to have lots of weird birds.

So yeh. Have some birds.
